lean_stdlib_notes.no_by_contra: by_contra is 'unknown tactic' in Lean 4.28.0 - use Nat.le_of_not_lt instead
lean_stdlib_notes.no_push_neg: push_neg not available without Mathlib - use Nat.not_le.mp or omega
lean_stdlib_notes.no_split_ifs: split_ifs not available without Mathlib - use by_cases h : cond
lean_stdlib_notes.no_nat_le_or_lt: Nat.le_or_lt does NOT exist in Lean 4.29 - use 'by_cases h : a <= b' instead
lean_stdlib_notes.omega_cant_min_max: omega cannot reason about Nat.min/max directly (treats as uninterpreted) - use Nat.le_max_left, Nat.max_le.mpr, Nat.min_le_left, Nat.min_le_right, Nat.min_eq_left/right, Nat.max_eq_left/right
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_min_pattern: For min bounds: use Nat.min_le_left/right. For equality: use Nat.min_eq_left (h: a<=b) : min a b = a. For le: use by_cases + Nat.min_eq_left/right + calc
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_max_le_mpr: Nat.max_le.mpr <h1, h2> proves max a b <= k from h1 : a <= k and h2 : b <= k
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_le_max_left: Nat.le_max_left a b : a <= max a b
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_max_eq_left: Nat.max_eq_left (h : b <= a) : max a b = a
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_max_eq_right: Nat.max_eq_right (h : a <= b) : max a b = b
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_min_le_left: Nat.min_le_left a b : min a b <= a
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_min_le_right: Nat.min_le_right a b : min a b <= b
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_not_le_mp: Nat.not_le.mp h where h : NOT(a <= b) gives b < a
lean_stdlib_notes.nat_le_of_lt: Nat.le_of_lt h where h : a < b gives a <= b
lean_stdlib_notes.barrel_file: formal-verification/lean/FVSquad.lean must import ALL module files for lake build to compile them
lean_stdlib_notes.import_placement: imports MUST be at the very top of the file, before any doc comments or code
lean_stdlib_notes.theorem_ordering: Define theorems BEFORE use - cannot forward-reference.
lean_stdlib_notes.match_vs_if_not: Use 'match matchTerm s.log idx term with | false => ... | true => ...' instead of 'if !matchTerm ...' for cleaner proofs. simp [f, h] where h : matchTerm ... = true/false cleanly unfolds.
lean_stdlib_notes.have_omega_for_neg: For ¬(a >= b) proofs needed in if_neg: use 'have hnotge : ¬(a <= b) := by omega' then rw [if_neg hne, if_neg hnotge]
lean_stdlib_notes.simp_handles_if_false: simp [h] where h : matchTerm ... = true resolves 'if matchTerm ... = false then none else ...' to the else branch. Also resolves prop 'conflict = 0' with hfc.
lean_stdlib_notes.prop_if_cleaner: Use Prop equality 'if conflict = 0 then ...' rather than Bool 'if conflict == 0 then ...' for cleaner proofs with if_neg/if_pos
lean_stdlib_notes.simp_maybe_append_pattern: simp [maybeAppend, h] resolves match + conflict branches together; then use order lemmas for max/min; for persisted: use have + omega + rw [if_neg]

critique_notes.main_semantic_gap: Voter lists use List Nat instead of deduplicated Finset/HashSet - theorems hold under implicit Nodup assumption not formally enforced
critique_notes.jci_empty_divergence: JCI9/JCI10: empty-config returns 0 in Lean vs u64::MAX in Rust - may mislead for membership-transition configs
